content.content:
593	pub struct GlobalState {
594	    /// Phase-B hook for the Lua text parser. Set by the embedder (`lua-cli`
595	    /// or stdlib host) to bridge the cyclic crate split between `lua-vm` and
596	    /// `lua-parse`: when `f_parser` decides the chunk is text, it invokes
597	    /// this hook instead of the parser stub. `None` leaves the stub in place
598	    /// so unit tests that never load text still work.
599	    pub parser_hook: Option<ParserHook>,
600	
601	    /// Phase-B hook for reading a Lua source file from disk. Set by `lua-cli`
602	    /// (or any embedder that wants `require`/`loadfile` to reach the file
603	    /// system) since `std::fs` is banned in `lua-stdlib`. `None` makes
604	    /// `loadfile` and the Lua-file searcher report a file-not-found error.
605	    pub file_loader_hook: Option<FileLoaderHook>,
606	
607	    // C: l_mem totalbytes — Phase D memory accounting
608	    // types.tsv: global_State.totalbytes → isize
609	    pub totalbytes: isize,
610	
611	    // C: l_mem GCdebt — Phase D GC pacing
612	    // types.tsv: global_State.GCdebt → isize
613	    pub gc_debt: isize,
614	
615	    // C: lu_mem GCestimate — Phase D
616	    pub gc_estimate: usize,
617	
618	    // C: lu_mem lastatomic — Phase D
619	    // types.tsv: global_State.lastatomic → usize
620	    pub lastatomic: usize,
621	
622	    // C: stringtable strt — intern table for short strings
623	    // types.tsv: global_State.strt → StringPool
624	    pub strt: StringPool,
625	
626	    // C: TValue l_registry — the Lua registry (always a table once state is complete)
627	    // types.tsv: global_State.l_registry → LuaValue
628	    pub l_registry: LuaValue,
629	
630	    // PORT NOTE (phase-b-reconcile): The lua-types LuaTable placeholder has
631	    // no storage, so we cannot persist `registry[LUA_RIDX_GLOBALS] = globals`
632	    // via the canonical registry path. Until the placeholder reconciles with
633	    // lua-vm::table::LuaTable, the globals table lives in a direct field
634	    // and `get_global_table` reads it from here. Same for `loaded` (the
635	    // module cache normally at `registry[_LOADED]`).
636	    pub globals: LuaValue,
637	    pub loaded: LuaValue,
638	
639	    // C: TValue nilvalue — nil sentinel; non-nil signals state not yet fully built
640	    // types.tsv: global_State.nilvalue → LuaValue
641	    // PORT NOTE: In Rust we use a dedicated `is_complete: bool` flag rather than
642	    // the C trick of checking `ttisnil(&g->nilvalue)`. See `is_complete()`.
643	    pub nilvalue: LuaValue,
644	
645	    // C: unsigned int seed — randomized seed for hashes
646	    // types.tsv: global_State.seed → u32
647	    pub seed: u32,
648	
649	    // C: lu_byte currentwhite — Phase D GC color
650	    // types.tsv: global_State.currentwhite → u8
651	    pub currentwhite: u8,
652	
653	    // C: lu_byte gcstate — Phase D GC FSM state
654	    pub gcstate: u8,
655	
656	    // C: lu_byte gckind — Phase D KGC_INC vs KGC_GEN
657	    pub gckind: u8,
658	
659	    // C: lu_byte gcstopem — Phase D
660	    pub gcstopem: bool,
661	
662	    // C: lu_byte genminormul — Phase D generational tuning
663	    // types.tsv: global_State.genminormul → u8
664	    pub genminormul: u8,
665	
666	    // C: lu_byte genmajormul — Phase D
667	    pub genmajormul: u8,
668	
669	    // C: lu_byte gcstp — controls GC running (GCSTPGC etc.)
670	    pub gcstp: u8,
671	
672	    // C: lu_byte gcemergency — Phase D emergency collection flag
673	    pub gcemergency: bool,
674	
675	    // C: lu_byte gcpause — pause size between GCs (/4 stored)
676	    // types.tsv: global_State.gcpause → u8
677	    pub gcpause: u8,
678	
679	    // C: lu_byte gcstepmul — GC speed (/4 stored)
680	    // types.tsv: global_State.gcstepmul → u8
681	    pub gcstepmul: u8,
682	
683	    // C: lu_byte gcstepsize — log2 of GC granularity
684	    pub gcstepsize: u8,
685	
686	    // C: GCObject *allgc — all collectable objects; Phase A–C leaks (Rc cycles)
687	    // types.tsv: global_State.allgc → Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>
688	    pub allgc: Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>,
689	
690	    // C: GCObject **sweepgc — sweep cursor into allgc; Phase D
691	    // types.tsv: global_State.sweepgc → (removed); replaced by usize cursor
692	    pub sweepgc_cursor: usize,
693	
694	    // C: GCObject *finobj — finalizable objects; Phase D
695	    // types.tsv: global_State.finobj → Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>
696	    pub finobj: Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>,
697	
698	    // C: GCObject *gray — GC gray list; Phase D
699	    // types.tsv: global_State.gray → Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>
700	    pub gray: Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>,
701	
702	    // C: GCObject *grayagain — Phase D
703	    pub grayagain: Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>,
704	
705	    // C: GCObject *weak — weak-value tables; Phase D
706	    // types.tsv: global_State.weak → Vec<GcRef<LuaTable>>
707	    pub weak: Vec<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
708	
709	    // C: GCObject *ephemeron — ephemeron tables; Phase D
710	    pub ephemeron: Vec<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
711	
712	    // C: GCObject *allweak — all-weak tables; Phase D
713	    pub allweak: Vec<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
714	
715	    // C: GCObject *tobefnz — pending finalizers; Phase D
716	    // types.tsv: global_State.tobefnz → Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>
717	    pub tobefnz: Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>,
718	
719	    // C: GCObject *fixedgc — non-collectable objects (reserved-word strings, etc.)
720	    // types.tsv: global_State.fixedgc → Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>
721	    pub fixedgc: Vec<GcRef<dyn Collectable>>,
722	
723	    // Generational cohort markers — Phase D only
724	    // types.tsv: global_State.survival/old1/reallyold/firstold1/finobjsur/finobjold1/finobjrold
725	    //   → (removed; replaced by index cursors in Phase D)
726	
727	    // C: struct lua_State *twups — threads with open upvalues
728	    // types.tsv: global_State.twups → Vec<GcRef<LuaState>>
729	    pub twups: Vec<GcRef<LuaState>>,
730	
731	    // C: lua_CFunction panic — panic handler; Phase B
732	    // types.tsv: global_State.panic → Option<lua_CFunction>
733	    pub panic: Option<LuaCFunction>,
734	
735	    // C: struct lua_State *mainthread
736	    // types.tsv: global_State.mainthread → GcRef<LuaState>
737	    // TODO(port): self-referential Rc cycle; Phase D GC handles cycles properly
738	    pub mainthread: Option<GcRef<LuaState>>,
739	
740	    // C: TString *memerrmsg — preallocated OOM error message
741	    // types.tsv: global_State.memerrmsg → GcRef<LuaString>
742	    pub memerrmsg: GcRef<LuaString>,
743	
744	    // C: TString *tmname[TM_N] — tag-method names indexed by TMS enum
745	    // types.tsv: global_State.tmname → [GcRef<LuaString>; TM_N]
746	    // TODO(port): TM_N constant and TagMethod enum come from ltm.c → tagmethods.rs
747	    pub tmname: Vec<GcRef<LuaString>>,
748	
749	    // C: struct Table *mt[LUA_NUMTYPES] — per-type metatables
750	    // types.tsv: global_State.mt → [Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>; LUA_NUMTYPES]
751	    pub mt: [Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>; LUA_NUMTYPES],
752	
753	    // C: TString *strcache[STRCACHE_N][STRCACHE_M] — string cache for luaS_new
754	    // types.tsv: global_State.strcache → [[GcRef<LuaString>; STRCACHE_M]; STRCACHE_N]
755	    pub strcache: [[GcRef<LuaString>; STRCACHE_M]; STRCACHE_N],
756	
757	    /// Stable intern map for the public [`LuaString`] type. Distinct from
758	    /// `strt` (which keys internal `LuaStringImpl`) because the parser and
759	    /// stdlib need pointer-equality across `intern_str` calls so
760	    /// `GcRef::ptr_eq` can resolve variable identity. Without this map each
761	    /// call allocates a fresh `GcRef` and locals/upvalues fail to resolve.
762	    pub interned_lt: std::collections::HashMap<Box<[u8]>, GcRef<LuaString>>,
763	
764	    // C: lua_WarnFunction warnf — warning function sink
765	    // types.tsv: global_State.warnf → Option<Box<dyn FnMut(&[u8], bool)>>
766	    pub warnf: Option<Box<dyn FnMut(&[u8], bool)>>,
767	    // C: void *ud_warn — folded into the `warnf` closure capture; removed
768	
769	    /// Registry of native `LuaCFunction` pointers. Lua-types cannot reference
770	    /// `LuaState`, so `LuaClosure::LightC` carries a `usize` index into this
771	    /// vector instead of the real function pointer. `push_c_function`
772	    /// registers the function and stores the resulting index in the closure.
773	    pub c_functions: Vec<LuaCFunction>,
774	
775	    /// Phase-D heap. Owns the allgc intrusive list and runs collections.
776	    /// During Phase A-C this is `paused=true`, so allocations don't auto-
777	    /// register and `step` is a no-op. Phase D-1d wires `unpause()` after
778	    /// state initialization, at which point `step` runs during VM dispatch.
779	    pub heap: lua_gc::Heap,
780	}
781	
782	impl GlobalState {
783	    /// Total live bytes allocated (GCdebt + totalbytes).
784	    ///
785	    /// C: `gettotalbytes(g)` macro → `cast(lu_mem, (g)->totalbytes + (g)->GCdebt)`
786	    /// macros.tsv: `gettotalbytes → g.total_bytes()`
787	    pub fn total_bytes(&self) -> usize {
788	        // C: cast(lu_mem, (g)->totalbytes + (g)->GCdebt)
789	        (self.totalbytes + self.gc_debt) as usize
790	    }
791	
792	    /// Returns `true` when the state has been fully initialized.
793	    ///
794	    /// C: `completestate(g)` macro → `ttisnil(&g->nilvalue)`
795	    /// macros.tsv: `completestate → g.is_complete()`
796	    ///
797	    /// PORT NOTE: C uses `g->nilvalue` being nil as the "complete" signal.
798	    /// We replicate the same logic: `nilvalue == Nil` means complete.
799	    pub fn is_complete(&self) -> bool {
800	        // C: ttisnil(&g->nilvalue)
801	        matches!(self.nilvalue, LuaValue::Nil)
802	    }
803	
804	    /// Returns the "current white" GC color bitmask.
805	    ///
806	    /// C: `luaC_white(g)` macro.
807	    /// macros.tsv: `luaC_white → g.current_white()`
808	    ///
809	    /// PORT NOTE: GC color management deferred to Phase D; always returns
810	    /// the initial white bit.
811	    pub fn current_white(&self) -> u8 {
812	        self.currentwhite
813	    }
814	
815	    /// Returns the "other white" GC color bitmask.
816	    ///
817	    /// C: `otherwhite(g)` macro.
818	    /// macros.tsv: `otherwhite → g.other_white()`
819	    pub fn other_white(&self) -> u8 {
820	        // TODO(port): Phase D — toggle white bit properly
821	        self.currentwhite ^ 0x03
822	    }
823	
824	    /// Returns `true` if the GC is in generational mode.
825	    ///
826	    /// C: `isdecGCmodegen(g)` macro.
827	    /// macros.tsv: `isdecGCmodegen → g.is_gen_mode()`
828	    pub fn is_gen_mode(&self) -> bool {
829	        self.gckind == GcKind::Generational as u8
830	    }
831	
832	    /// Returns `true` if the GC is currently running.
833	    ///
834	    /// C: `gcrunning(g)` macro.
835	    /// macros.tsv: `gcrunning → g.gc_running()`
836	    pub fn gc_running(&self) -> bool {
837	        self.gcstp == 0
838	    }
839	
840	    /// Returns `true` while the GC is in its propagation phase.
841	    ///
842	    /// C: `keepinvariant(g)` macro.
843	    /// macros.tsv: `keepinvariant → g.keep_invariant()`
844	    pub fn keep_invariant(&self) -> bool {
845	        // TODO(port): Phase D — check gcstate for propagation phases
846	        false
847	    }
848	
849	    /// Returns `true` while the GC is in a sweep phase.
850	    ///
851	    /// C: `issweepphase(g)` macro.
852	    /// macros.tsv: `issweepphase → g.is_sweep_phase()`
853	    pub fn is_sweep_phase(&self) -> bool {
854	        // TODO(port): Phase D — check gcstate for sweep states (GCSswpallgc etc.)
855	        false
856	    }
857	
858	    // ── Phase-B stubs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
859	    pub fn gc_debt(&self) -> isize { self.gc_debt }
860	    pub fn set_gc_debt(&mut self, d: isize) { self.gc_debt = d; }
861	    pub fn gc_at_pause(&self) -> bool { self.gcstate == 0 }
862	    pub fn gc_pause_param(&self) -> u8 { self.gcpause }
863	    pub fn set_gc_pause_param(&mut self, p: u8) { self.gcpause = p; }
864	    pub fn gc_stepmul_param(&self) -> u8 { self.gcstepmul }
865	    pub fn set_gc_stepmul_param(&mut self, p: u8) { self.gcstepmul = p; }
866	    pub fn set_gc_genmajormul(&mut self, p: u8) { self.genmajormul = p; }
867	    pub fn gc_stop_flags(&self) -> u8 { self.gcstp }
868	    pub fn set_gc_stop_flags(&mut self, f: u8) { self.gcstp = f; }
869	    pub fn set_gc_stop_user(&mut self) { todo!("phase-b: set_gc_stop_user") }
870	    pub fn clear_gc_stop(&mut self) { self.gcstp = 0; }
871	    pub fn is_gc_stopped_internally(&self) -> bool { self.gcstp != 0 }
872	    pub fn tm_name<T>(&self, _tm: T) -> Option<GcRef<LuaString>> { todo!("phase-b: tm_name") }
873	}

875	use lua_types::tagmethod::TagMethod;
876	
877	// ─── LuaState ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
878	
879	/// Per-thread Lua execution state.
880	///
881	/// C: `struct lua_State` in `lstate.h`.
882	/// types.tsv: `lua_State → LuaState`
883	///
884	/// All stack-pointer fields in C (`StkIdRel`, `StkId`) become `StackIdx` (u32
885	/// index into `stack: Vec<StackValue>`).  The C intrusive `CallInfo` linked list
886	/// becomes `call_info: Vec<CallInfo>` indexed by `CallInfoIdx`.
887	pub struct LuaState {
888	    // ── Thread status ──
889	
890	    // C: lu_byte status — thread status (LUA_OK / LUA_YIELD / LUA_ERR*)
891	    // types.tsv: lua_State.status → u8
892	    pub status: u8,

887	pub struct LuaState {
888	    // ── Thread status ──
889	
890	    // C: lu_byte status — thread status (LUA_OK / LUA_YIELD / LUA_ERR*)
891	    // types.tsv: lua_State.status → u8
892	    pub status: u8,
893	
894	    // C: lu_byte allowhook — hook-enabled flag
895	    // types.tsv: lua_State.allowhook → bool
896	    pub allowhook: bool,
897	
898	    // C: unsigned short nci — number of CallInfo entries in use
899	    // types.tsv: lua_State.nci → u32
900	    pub nci: u32,
901	
902	    // ── Stack ──
903	
904	    // C: StkIdRel top — first free stack slot
905	    // types.tsv: lua_State.top → StackIdx
906	    pub top: StackIdx,
907	
908	    // C: StkIdRel stack_last — end-of-stack sentinel (stack.p + BASIC_STACK_SIZE)
909	    // types.tsv: lua_State.stack_last → StackIdx (redundant once Vec; kept for parity)
910	    pub stack_last: StackIdx,
911	
912	    // C: StkIdRel stack — the stack base pointer; in Rust this is the Vec itself
913	    // types.tsv: lua_State.stack → Vec<StackValue>
914	    pub stack: Vec<StackValue>,
915	
916	    // ── Call info ──
917	
918	    // C: CallInfo *ci — current call frame; raw pointer in C
919	    // types.tsv: lua_State.ci → CallInfoIdx
920	    pub ci: CallInfoIdx,
921	
922	    // C: CallInfo base_ci — bottom CallInfo (C→Lua entry); element 0 of the Vec
923	    // types.tsv: lua_State.base_ci → CallInfo  (Vec element 0)
924	    // PORT NOTE: In Rust, base_ci is call_info[0]. There is no separate field.
925	    pub call_info: Vec<CallInfo>,
926	
927	    // ── Upvalues / to-be-closed ──
928	
929	    // C: UpVal *openupval — open upvalue list (was intrusive; now a Vec)
930	    // types.tsv: lua_State.openupval → Vec<GcRef<UpVal>>
931	    pub openupval: Vec<GcRef<UpVal>>,
932	
933	    // C: StkIdRel tbclist — to-be-closed list (was StkIdRel pointer; now Vec of idx)
934	    // types.tsv: lua_State.tbclist → Vec<StackIdx>
935	    pub tbclist: Vec<StackIdx>,
936	
937	    // ── Global state ──
938	
939	    // C: global_State *l_G — pointer to shared GlobalState
940	    // types.tsv: lua_State.l_G → (accessed via method)
941	    // PORT NOTE: Rc<RefCell<>> for shared ownership across coroutine threads.
942	    pub(crate) global: Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>>,
943	
944	    // ── Hooks ──
945	
946	    // C: volatile lua_Hook hook
947	    // types.tsv: lua_State.hook → Option<Box<dyn FnMut(&mut LuaState, &LuaDebug)>>
948	    // TODO(port): LuaDebug defined in ldebug.c → debug.rs (Phase E)
949	    pub hook: Option<Box<dyn Fn()>>,
950	
951	    // C: volatile l_signalT hookmask
952	    // types.tsv: lua_State.hookmask → u8
953	    pub hookmask: u8,
954	
955	    // C: int basehookcount
956	    // types.tsv: lua_State.basehookcount → i32
957	    pub basehookcount: i32,
958	
959	    // C: int hookcount
960	    // types.tsv: lua_State.hookcount → i32
961	    pub hookcount: i32,
962	
963	    // ── Error handling ──
964	
965	    // C: struct lua_longjmp *errorJmp — C longjmp recovery point
966	    // types.tsv: lua_State.errorJmp → (removed; replaced by Result<T, LuaError>)
967	    // PORT NOTE: Entirely removed. The `?` operator replaces setjmp/longjmp.
968	
969	    // C: ptrdiff_t errfunc — error-handler stack position (0 = none)
970	    // types.tsv: lua_State.errfunc → isize
971	    pub errfunc: isize,
972	
973	    // ── C-call depth ──
974	
975	    // C: l_uint32 nCcalls — packed (recursion_count | non_yieldable_count << 16)
976	    // types.tsv: lua_State.nCcalls → u32
977	    pub nCcalls: u32,
978	
979	    // ── Debug / hooks ──
980	
981	    // C: int oldpc — last pc traced (for hooks)
982	    // types.tsv: lua_State.oldpc → u32
983	    pub oldpc: u32,
984	
985	    // ── GC color (Phase D) ──
986	
987	    // C: lu_byte marked — GC color/age bits; Phase D only
988	    // types.tsv: GCObject.marked → u8
989	    pub marked: u8,
990	}
991	
992	impl LuaState {
993	    /// Access the process-wide `GlobalState` immutably.
994	    ///
995	    /// C: `G(L)` macro → `state.global()`.
996	    /// macros.tsv: `G → state.global()`
997	    ///
998	    /// PORT NOTE: Returns `std::cell::Ref<GlobalState>` because GlobalState is held in
999	    /// `Rc<RefCell<...>>`. Call sites that do `state.global().field` should work fine
1000	    /// via `Deref`. Callers must not hold the `Ref` across a `global_mut()` call.
1001	    pub fn global(&self) -> std::cell::Ref<'_, GlobalState> {
1002	        self.global.borrow()
1003	    }
1004	
1005	    /// Access the process-wide `GlobalState` mutably.
1006	    ///
1007	    /// C: `G(L)` + indirect write → `state.global_mut()`.
1008	    /// macros.tsv: `G → state.global()` (writes use `state.global_mut()`)
1009	    pub fn global_mut(&self) -> std::cell::RefMut<'_, GlobalState> {
1010	        self.global.borrow_mut()
1011	    }
1012	
1013	    /// Clone the `Rc` handle to the GlobalState for sharing with a new coroutine.
1014	    ///
1015	    /// Used in `new_thread` to give the child thread access to the same GlobalState.
1016	    pub fn global_rc(&self) -> Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>> {
1017	        Rc::clone(&self.global)
1018	    }
1019	
1020	    /// Return the current C-call recursion depth (lower 16 bits of `nCcalls`).
1021	    ///
1022	    /// C: `getCcalls(L)` macro → `(L)->nCcalls & 0xffff`
1023	    /// macros.tsv: `getCcalls → state.c_calls()`
1024	    pub fn c_calls(&self) -> u32 {
1025	        self.nCcalls & 0xffff
1026	    }
1027	
1028	    /// Increment the non-yieldable call count (upper 16 bits of `nCcalls`).
1029	    ///
1030	    /// C: `incnny(L)` macro → `(L)->nCcalls += 0x10000`
1031	    /// macros.tsv: `incnny → state.inc_nny()`
1032	    pub fn inc_nny(&mut self) {
1033	        self.nCcalls += 0x10000;
1034	    }
1035	
1036	    /// Decrement the non-yieldable call count.
1037	    ///
1038	    /// C: `decnny(L)` macro → `(L)->nCcalls -= 0x10000`
1039	    /// macros.tsv: `decnny → state.dec_nny()`
1040	    pub fn dec_nny(&mut self) {
1041	        self.nCcalls -= 0x10000;
1042	    }
1043	
1044	    /// Returns `true` if the thread can yield (no non-yieldable frames on the stack).
1045	    ///
1046	    /// C: `yieldable(L)` macro → `((L)->nCcalls & 0xffff0000) == 0`
1047	    /// macros.tsv: `yieldable → state.is_yieldable()`
1048	    pub fn is_yieldable(&self) -> bool {
1049	        (self.nCcalls & 0xffff0000) == 0
1050	    }
1051	
1052	    /// Reset the hook countdown to the baseline.
1053	    ///
1054	    /// C: `resethookcount(L)` macro → `L->hookcount = L->basehookcount`
1055	    /// macros.tsv: `resethookcount → state.reset_hook_count()`
1056	    pub fn reset_hook_count(&mut self) {
1057	        self.hookcount = self.basehookcount;
1058	    }
1059	
1060	    /// Returns the current stack capacity (slots between base and stack_last).
1061	    ///
1062	    /// C: `stacksize(th)` macro → `cast_int((th)->stack_last.p - (th)->stack.p)`
1063	    /// macros.tsv: `stacksize → state.stack_size()`
1064	    pub fn stack_size(&self) -> usize {
1065	        self.stack_last.0 as usize
1066	    }
1067	
1068	    /// Push a value onto the stack, incrementing `top`.
1069	    ///
1070	    /// C: `*L->top++ = val` (various push patterns)
1071	    /// macros.tsv: `api_incr_top → gone — state.push() already increments`
1072	    pub fn push(&mut self, val: LuaValue) {
1073	        let top = self.top.0 as usize;
1074	        if top < self.stack.len() {
1075	            self.stack[top] = StackValue { val, tbc_delta: 0 };
1076	        } else {
1077	            self.stack.push(StackValue { val, tbc_delta: 0 });
1078	        }
1079	        self.top = StackIdx(self.top.0 + 1);
1080	    }
1081	
1082	    /// Pop the top value from the stack, decrementing `top`.
1083	    ///
1084	    /// C: `L->top--` + dereference.
1085	    pub fn pop(&mut self) -> LuaValue {
1086	        if self.top.0 == 0 {
1087	            return LuaValue::Nil;
1088	        }
1089	        self.top = StackIdx(self.top.0 - 1);
1090	        self.stack[self.top.0 as usize].val.clone()
1091	    }
1092	
1093	    /// Retrieve the value at the given stack index without removing it.
1094	    ///
1095	    /// C: `s2v(L->stack.p + idx)` / stack slot access.
1096	    /// macros.tsv: `s2v → state.stack_at(idx)` → returns `&LuaValue`
1097	    pub fn stack_val(&self, idx: StackIdx) -> &LuaValue {
1098	        &self.stack[idx.0 as usize].val
1099	    }
1100	
1101	    /// Write a value to a specific stack slot.
1102	    pub fn set_stack_val(&mut self, idx: StackIdx, val: LuaValue) {
1103	        self.stack[idx.0 as usize].val = val;
1104	    }
1105	
1106	    /// Returns a no-op GC handle.
1107	    ///
1108	    /// C: Various `luaC_*` calls → `state.gc().*`
1109	    /// macros.tsv: `luaC_checkGC → state.gc().check_step()`, etc.
1110	    ///
1111	    /// PORT NOTE: In Phases A–C the GC is `Rc`-based and all GC operations are
1112	    /// no-ops. Phase D replaces this with real GC logic in `lua-gc`.
1113	    pub fn gc(&mut self) -> GcHandle<'_> {
1114	        GcHandle { _state: self }
1115	    }
1116	
1117	    /// Create a new empty table and register it with the GC.
1118	    ///
1119	    /// C: `luaH_new(L)` → `state.new_table()` returning `GcRef<LuaTable>`
1120	    /// macros.tsv: `lua_newtable → state.new_table()`
1121	    pub fn new_table(&mut self) -> GcRef<LuaTable> {
1122	        // TODO(port): register with GC tracking (state.global_mut().allgc) in Phase D
1123	        GcRef::new(LuaTable::placeholder())
1124	    }
1125	
1126	    /// Intern a byte string in the global string pool.
1127	    ///
1128	    /// C: `luaS_new(L, s)` → `state.intern_str(s: &[u8])`
1129	    /// macros.tsv: `luaS_new → state.intern_str(s)`
1130	    pub fn intern_str(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<GcRef<LuaString>, LuaError> {
1131	        if let Some(existing) = self.global().interned_lt.get(bytes) {
1132	            return Ok(existing.clone());
1133	        }
1134	        let _local = crate::string::new(self, bytes)?;
1135	        let new_ref = GcRef::new(LuaString::from_bytes(bytes.to_vec()));
1136	        self.global_mut()
1137	            .interned_lt
1138	            .insert(bytes.to_vec().into_boxed_slice(), new_ref.clone());
1139	        Ok(new_ref)
1140	    }
1141	
1142	    /// Returns the current CallInfo index (the active call frame).
1143	    pub fn top_idx(&self) -> StackIdx {
1144	        self.top
1145	    }
1146	}
1147	
1148	// ─── Phase-B stub methods ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1149	//
1150	// The methods in the impl blocks below were referenced by api.rs, debug.rs,
1151	// do_.rs, vm.rs, tagmethods.rs etc. during Phase A. Each body is a `todo!()`
1152	// pinned to a phase-b task; once the corresponding C function is faithfully
1153	// ported the stub will be replaced. Signatures are inferred from call sites
1154	// and should be treated as Phase-B-grade approximations.
1155	
1156	impl LuaState {
1157	    pub fn get_at(&self, idx: impl Into<StackIdxConv>) -> LuaValue {
1158	        let i: StackIdx = idx.into().0;
1159	        match self.stack.get(i.0 as usize) {
1160	            Some(slot) => slot.val.clone(),
1161	            None => LuaValue::Nil,
1162	        }
1163	    }
1164	    pub fn set_at(&mut self, idx: impl Into<StackIdxConv>, v: LuaValue) {
1165	        let i: StackIdx = idx.into().0;
1166	        self.stack[i.0 as usize].val = v;
1167	    }
1168	    /// Set `top` to an absolute stack index. Grows the backing stack vector
1169	    /// (filling new slots with `Nil`) when `idx` is past `stack.len()`, but
1170	    /// never clobbers existing slots between the old top and the new top —
1171	    /// VM opcodes (Call, ForPrep, etc.) write registers via `set_at` and then
1172	    /// raise `top` to signal "these are now live"; nil-filling here would
1173	    /// erase the just-written values.
1174	    ///
1175	    /// C: internal `L->top.p = newtop` assignment. The `for (; diff > 0; …)
1176	    /// setnilvalue(s2v(L->top.p++))` clear loop in `lua_settop` (lapi.c) is
1177	    /// part of the public API path and lives in `api::set_top` instead.
1178	    /// PORT NOTE: callers pass an absolute `StackIdx`, not the relative `idx`
1179	    /// of the public `lua_settop`. The to-be-closed (`tbclist`) close path
1180	    /// is Phase E and not handled here.
1181	    pub fn set_top(&mut self, idx: impl Into<StackIdxConv>) {
1182	        let new_top: StackIdx = idx.into().0;
1183	        let new_top_u = new_top.0 as usize;
1184	        if new_top_u > self.stack.len() {
1185	            self.stack.resize_with(new_top_u, StackValue::default);
1186	        }

563	// ─── GlobalState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
564	
565	/// Function-pointer signature for the text-source parser, installed on
566	/// [`GlobalState::parser_hook`] by the embedder.
567	///
568	/// The implementation lives in `lua-parse`; `lua-vm` cannot depend on it
569	/// directly (that would form a cycle), so the parser is reached via this
570	/// function pointer registered at startup.
571	pub type ParserHook = fn(
572	    state: &mut LuaState,
573	    source: &[u8],
574	    name: &[u8],
575	    firstchar: i32,
576	) -> Result<GcRef<lua_types::closure::LuaLClosure>, LuaError>;
577	
578	/// Function-pointer signature for reading a file's full contents into memory,
579	/// installed on [`GlobalState::file_loader_hook`] by the embedder.
580	///
581	/// `std::fs` is banned outside `lua-cli`, so `lua-stdlib`'s `loadfile` and
582	/// `searcher_lua` reach the filesystem via this hook. `None` keeps the file
583	/// system unreachable, which is appropriate for embeddings where modules are
584	/// served exclusively from `package.preload`.
585	pub type FileLoaderHook = fn(filename: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LuaError>;
586	
587	/// Process-wide state shared by all Lua threads.
588	///
589	/// C: `global_State` in `lstate.h`.
590	/// types.tsv: `global_State → GlobalState`
591	///
592	/// Not exposed directly at the API; accessed via `state.global()` / `state.global_mut()`.
593	pub struct GlobalState {
594	    /// Phase-B hook for the Lua text parser. Set by the embedder (`lua-cli`
595	    /// or stdlib host) to bridge the cyclic crate split between `lua-vm` and
596	    /// `lua-parse`: when `f_parser` decides the chunk is text, it invokes
597	    /// this hook instead of the parser stub. `None` leaves the stub in place
598	    /// so unit tests that never load text still work.
599	    pub parser_hook: Option<ParserHook>,

+        // C: `traversethread` in lgc.c walks the live portion of the stack
+        // (`stack..top`) and the open-upvalue list. Slots past `top` are
+        // dead and must not be visited.
+        // PORT NOTE: `global` (Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>>) is reached from the
+        // heap's root via GlobalState::trace; tracing it from each thread
+        // would re-enter the root and is explicitly excluded.
+        // PORT NOTE: `call_info` entries carry pc offsets and stack indices
+        // but no direct GcRef fields. The active closure is reached through
+        // the stack slot at `ci.func`, already covered by the stack walk.
+        // PORT NOTE: `tbclist` holds StackIdx values only; the to-be-closed
+        // objects themselves live on the stack and are traced there.
-        todo!("phase-d: trace GlobalState — l_registry, mainthread, strt pool, mt[..], tmname[..], memerrmsg, twups, fixedgc");
+        // C: `restartcollection` in lgc.c marks mainthread, l_registry, the
+        // per-type metatables, and pending finalizers. We expand the set to
+        // include preallocated short strings (memerrmsg, tmname[]) and the
+        // open-upvalue thread list, both of which the panic-driven Phase-D
+        // mega-loop expects to see at the root.
+        // PORT NOTE: `strt` (intern table) is a weak table in C; entries are
+        // cleared during the atomic weak-table pass (`clearbykeys`), not
+        // marked as roots. Same for `strcache` and `interned_lt`.
+        // PORT NOTE: `fixedgc` holds objects pre-marked fixed/black at
+        // allocation (`luaC_fix`); the mark phase never re-visits them, and
+        // `weak`, `ephemeron`, `allweak` are GC bookkeeping lists owned by
+        // `heap` — they are the universe of allocated objects, not roots.

ify it builds.

##

Report

**File / line:** `crates/lua-vm/src/trace_impls.rs:30` (the `impl Trace for G

lobalState` body).

**What it does:** Implements Phase-D root tracing for `GlobalState`. Visits the can
onical GC roots (`l_registry`, `mainthread`, per-type metatables `mt[..]`, tag-method names `tmname[..]
`, preallocated `memerrmsg`, and the open-upvalue thread list `twups`) so `Heap::full_collect` can re
ach every live object from one entry point. Weak tables (`strt`, `strcache`, `interned_lt`), the `fixedgc` pre-marked list, and
the heap-owned bookkeeping vectors are intentionally excluded with PORT NOTEs explaining why each

is not a root.

**Family members also implemented:**
- `impl Trace for LuaState` (same file, sibling
Trace impl): walks the live stack slots `stack[..top]` and the `openupval
` vector; deliberately does not trace the `global` `Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>>`
per the original stub's instruction, and documents why `call_info`/`tbclist`

carry no direct GcRef payload.

**Newly-surfaced todos notic
ed but NOT implemented:** every child `Trace` impl invoked through `GcRef
<T>` auto-deref (`LuaValue`, `LuaString`, `LuaTable`, `UpVal`,
`LuaProto`, `LuaClosure`, `LuaLClosure`) is still a `todo!()` in `crates/lua-types/src/trace_impls.rs`. These will
fire one-at-a-time when `full_collect` actually runs over a non-trivial state — exactly the pan
ic-driven sequencing the file's own doc-comment prescribes ("each agent works on

ONE type — no family expansion"). I therefore did not touch them in this session.

**`
cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only, all pre-existing).

## Report

**File / line:** `crates/lua-vm/src/trace_impls.rs:30` (the `impl Trace for GlobalState` body).

**What it does:** Implements Phase-D root tracing for `GlobalState`. Visits the canonical GC roots (`l_registry`, `mainthread`, per-type metatables `mt[..]`, tag-method names `tmname[..]`, preallocated `memerrmsg`, and the open-upvalue thread list `twups`) so `Heap::full_collect` can reach every live object from one entry point. Weak tables (`strt`, `strcache`, `interned_lt`), the `fixedgc` pre-marked list, and the heap-owned bookkeeping vectors are intentionally excluded with PORT NOTEs explaining why each is not a root.

**Family members also implemented:**
- `impl Trace for LuaState` (same file, sibling Trace impl): walks the live stack slots `stack[..top]` and the `openupval` vector; deliberately does not trace the `global` `Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>>` per the original stub's instruction, and documents why `call_info`/`tbclist` carry no direct GcRef payload.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but NOT implemented:** every child `Trace` impl invoked through `GcRef<T>` auto-deref (`LuaValue`, `LuaString`, `LuaTable`, `UpVal`, `LuaProto`, `LuaClosure`, `LuaLClosure`) is still a `todo!()` in `crates/lua-types/src/trace_impls.rs`. These will fire one-at-a-time when `full_collect` actually runs over a non-trivial state — exactly the panic-driven sequencing the file's own doc-comment prescribes ("each agent works on ONE type — no family expansion"). I therefore did not touch them in this session.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only, all pre-existing).

## Report

**File / line:** `crates/lua-vm/src/trace_impls.rs:30` (the `impl Trace for GlobalState` body).

**What it does:** Implements Phase-D root tracing for `GlobalState`. Visits the canonical GC roots (`l_registry`, `mainthread`, per-type metatables `mt[..]`, tag-method names `tmname[..]`, preallocated `memerrmsg`, and the open-upvalue thread list `twups`) so `Heap::full_collect` can reach every live object from one entry point. Weak tables (`strt`, `strcache`, `interned_lt`), the `fixedgc` pre-marked list, and the heap-owned bookkeeping vectors are intentionally excluded with PORT NOTEs explaining why each is not a root.

**Family members also implemented:**
- `impl Trace for LuaState` (same file, sibling Trace impl): walks the live stack slots `stack[..top]` and the `openupval` vector; deliberately does not trace the `global` `Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>>` per the original stub's instruction, and documents why `call_info`/`tbclist` carry no direct GcRef payload.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but NOT implemented:** every child `Trace` impl invoked through `GcRef<T>` auto-deref (`LuaValue`, `LuaString`, `LuaTable`, `UpVal`, `LuaProto`, `LuaClosure`, `LuaLClosure`) is still a `todo!()` in `crates/lua-types/src/trace_impls.rs`. These will fire one-at-a-time when `full_collect` actually runs over a non-trivial state — exactly the panic-driven sequencing the file's own doc-comment prescribes ("each agent works on ONE type — no family expansion"). I therefore did not touch them in this session.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only, all pre-existing).
